title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Placed config directory. Sets default thresholds used datasets unless dataset overrides .
code: snapshot_db:       "data/snapshots.sqlite" report_output_dir: "reports/"  default_rules:   # Proportion of non-empty values that must parse as numeric for a column to   # be classified as numeric (affects type inference across many checks)   type_inference_threshold: 0.90    # FAIL if any column's missing-value rate exceeds this (0-1)   max_missing_rate: 0.05    # FAIL if non-numeric values in a numeric column exceed this rate   max_non_numeric_rate: 0.01    # FAIL if row count drops below this (0 = disabled)   min_row_count: 0    # Version-comparison thresholds (used when a previous file is available)   max_row_count_change_pct:       0.10   max_numeric_mean_shift_pct:     0.20   max_missing_rate_change_pp:     2.0    # percentage points   max_non_numeric_rate_change_pp: 1.0    flag_new_columns:         true   # set false to suppress new-column warnings in CP-02   flag_dropped_columns:     true   # set false to suppress dropped-column warnings in CP-02   flag_type_changes:        true   # set false to suppress type-change warnings in CP-02   flag_column_order_change: true   # set false to skip CP-08 entirely (WARN CSV / FAIL FWF)

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: One file per dataset. keys specify needed — everything else inherits global defaults.
code: dataset_name: "customer_accounts"   # for human reference only — the functional                                      # identifier is the YAML filename, which must                                      # match the dataset_name argument to run_dq_check()  # --- File location --- # Option A: folder scan (two most recently modified files are used) folder: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/"  # Option B: explicit paths (set current_file only → single-file mode, #           no version comparison) # current_file:  "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260410.csv" # previous_file: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260401.csv"  format:    csv encoding:  "UTF-8" delimiter: ","  # --- Schema contract (optional) --- # SC-01 flags extra columns; SC-02 flags missing columns. expected_columns:   - id   - name   - country_code   - account_status   - account_balance   - created_date  # --- Key columns (optional) --- # QC-12 checks each for duplicate values. key_columns:   - id  # --- Rule overrides (optional) --- # These replace the matching keys in default_rules for this dataset only. rule_overrides:   max_missing_rate:        0.02   max_row_count_change_pct: 0.05   min_row_count:           1000  # --- Per-column type overrides (optional) --- # Force specific columns to a type regardless of what the data looks like. # Valid values: character, numeric, date. # Eliminates false positives on phone numbers, postcodes, unit numbers, etc. column_types:   phone:    character   # prevent QC-11 / CP-04 / CP-07 false positives   postcode: character   # 4-digit codes are not meaningful as numeric   bsb_code: character   # 6-digit bank codes are not meaningful as numeric  # --- Per-column rules (optional) --- column_rules:   country_code:     allowed_values: ["GB", "US", "DE", "FR"]   # QC-09     pattern: "^[A-Z]{2}$"                       # QC-13   account_status:     allowed_values: ["ACTIVE", "CLOSED", "SUSPENDED"]   account_balance:     min_value: 0                                 # QC-10     max_value: 1000000   # Per-column threshold overrides (optional, within column_rules):   email:     max_missing_rate: 1.00          # email is 99% absent by design — suppress QC-01     max_missing_rate_change_pp: 5.0 # looser than global 2.0 — suppress CP-03 noise   account_balance:     max_missing_rate: 0.00          # balance must always be present     max_numeric_mean_shift_pct: 0.05  # stricter than global 0.20  # --- Custom checks (optional) --- # custom_checks_file: "custom/customer_accounts_checks.R"  # --- Output paths (optional, override global) --- # snapshot_db:       "data/snapshots.sqlite" # report_output_dir: "reports/"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: infer_col_type() classifies column whitespace trimming. Rules applied priority order: date — non-empty values parse known date format (%Y-%m-%d, %d/%m/%Y, %m/%d/%Y, %Y%m%d, %d-%m-%Y) numeric — ≥ type_inference_threshold non-empty values coerce numeric character — everything else unknown — values empty NA threshold defaults 90%, meaning column 10% bad values still classified numeric, making bad values visible QC-11 (non-numeric contamination). configurable per dataset via type_inference_threshold default_rules rule_overrides: Changing threshold affects QC-06, QC-07, QC-08, QC-11, CP-02, CP-04, CP-05, CP-06, CP-07. Note changing runs may cause CP-02 report spurious type change columns sit close old new thresholds, even underlying data change.
code: # raise threshold — stricter, fewer columns classified as numeric rule_overrides:   type_inference_threshold: 0.95  # lower threshold — more lenient, columns with more noise still classified numeric rule_overrides:   type_inference_threshold: 0.80 infer_col_type(c("2024-01-15", "2024-06-30"))  # "date" infer_col_type(c("100", "200", "N/A"))          # "character" (only 67% numeric) infer_col_type(c("100", "200", "N/A", rep("50", 17)))  # "numeric" (95% numeric) infer_col_type(c(NA, "", NA))                   # "unknown" # with a custom threshold: infer_col_type(c(rep("1", 17), "a", "b", "c"), threshold = 0.80)  # "numeric" (85%)

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: columns formatted numbers semantically character — phone numbers, postcodes, unit numbers, BSB codes. Automatic inference classifies numeric, triggers irrelevant QC-11, CP-04, CP-07 checks can cause spurious CP-02 type-change warnings proportion non-numeric values drifts across inference threshold deliveries. Use column_types dataset YAML force type column: Valid values character, numeric, date. override affects downstream logic: type stored SQLite column’s inferred_type, drift comparisons see stable override, QC/CP checks meaningless forced type skipped automatically. Within custom check scripts, declare config second argument use resolve_col_type() instead infer_col_type() overrides respected. function signature one argument (df), config passed — signatures work:
code: column_types:   TenantPhone:    character   # 10-digit numbers — not meaningful as numeric   PremisesUnit:   character   # values like "5", "3A", "Ground Floor"   PremisesPostCode: character # 4-digit codes validated by pattern, not numeric   BSBCode:        character   # 6-digit bank routing codes # Two-argument form — config is passed automatically, overrides respected custom_checks <- function(df, config) {   results <- list()   for (col in names(df)) {     if (resolve_col_type(col, df[[col]], config) == "numeric") {       # numeric-specific check logic here     }   }   results }  # One-argument form — still works, type inference only (no override awareness) custom_checks <- function(df) {   # ... }

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Global dataset-level thresholds apply uniformly columns. columns naturally higher missing rates variability, making single threshold produce constant noise. Per-column threshold keys column_rules let tune specific columns without affecting others. Resolution order every threshold-based check:
code: column_rules.<col>.<threshold>  →  rule_overrides.<threshold>  →  default_rules.<threshold>

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: threshold field HTML report dq_result objects reflects effective per-column threshold, global default, report always shows value actually used.
code: column_rules:   # Email is almost always absent by design — don't alert on it   LandlordEmail:     max_missing_rate:          1.00   # effectively disables QC-01 for this column     max_missing_rate_change_pp: 5.0   # allow larger swings without CP-03 noise    # Bond amount must always be present and should not shift much   BondAmount:     max_missing_rate:          0.00   # zero tolerance     max_numeric_mean_shift_pct: 0.05  # tighter than global 0.20    # Phone is forced to character via column_types, so numeric thresholds don't apply,   # but you can still tighten missing-rate expectations:   TenantPhone:     max_missing_rate: 0.10

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: HTML report contains: Header — dataset name, file name, run time, overall status badge File summary — row count, column count, file size, format, encoding Quality checks table — every check result, failures first Custom checks table — configured Version comparison — changed since previous delivery Historical trend — row count check outcomes across last 10 runs (appears least two runs recorded) Appendix — full column-level statistics table
code: result <- run_dq_check(   dataset_name = "customer_accounts",   config_dir   = "path/to/configs",   # contains dqcheckr.yml + customer_accounts.yml   open_report  = TRUE                 # open HTML in browser when interactive )  # Console output (always printed): # [dqcheckr] customer_accounts: FAIL - 1 warning(s), 2 failure(s). Report: reports/...html  result$status       # "PASS", "WARN", or "FAIL" result$report_path  # full path to the HTML file result$snapshot_id  # integer row ID in snapshots table

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: can call check function directly, without going full pipeline. Every check takes (df, config) returns list dq_result objects.
code: library(dqcheckr)  # Build a minimal config programmatically cfg <- list(   rules = list(     max_missing_rate     = 0.05,     max_non_numeric_rate = 0.01,     min_row_count        = 0   ),   column_rules     = list(     status = list(allowed_values = c("ACTIVE", "CLOSED"))   ),   key_columns      = "id",   expected_columns = NULL )  df <- read.csv("data/customer_accounts.csv", colClasses = "character") df[] <- lapply(df, trimws)  # Run a single check results <- check_missing_rate(df, cfg)  # Inspect results[[1]]$status    # "PASS" or "FAIL" results[[1]]$observed  # e.g. "3.2% missing (4 of 125)"  # Run all single-snapshot checks at once all_results <- run_qc_checks(df, cfg)  # Overall status overall_status(all_results)  # "FAIL" > "WARN" > "PASS" > "INFO"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Create plain .R file defines custom_checks(df). receives trimmed data frame must return list dq_result objects. Point dataset config: file sourced isolated environment (global session). dq_result() available inside . error — missing file, undefined function, runtime failure — stops run clear message. Tip: always set column = check scoped specific column. Results column = NA (default) appear HTML report contribute overall status, written column_snapshots SQLite table therefore appear per-column trend queries.
code: # File: custom/customer_accounts_checks.R  custom_checks <- function(df) {   results <- list()    # Rule: ACTIVE accounts must not have a zero balance   active_zero <- df[df$account_status == "ACTIVE" &                     !is.na(df$account_balance) &                     df$account_balance == "0", ]   n <- nrow(active_zero)   results <- c(results, list(dq_result(     check_id   = "CUST-01",     check_name = "No zero-balance active accounts",     column     = "account_balance",   # enables per-column storage in SQLite     status     = if (n > 0) "FAIL" else "PASS",     observed   = sprintf("%d ACTIVE account(s) with balance 0", n),     message    = if (n > 0)       sprintf("%d ACTIVE account(s) have a zero balance.", n)     else       "No ACTIVE accounts have a zero balance."   )))    results } custom_checks_file: "custom/customer_accounts_checks.R"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Every run writes two rows shared SQLite database: one row snapshots (run-level summary) one row per column-stat column_snapshots. datasets share one database, can query across : output/ directory (database file) created automatically first run exist.
code: library(DBI) library(RSQLite)  con <- dbConnect(SQLite(), "data/snapshots.sqlite")  # Recent runs for one dataset dbGetQuery(con,   "SELECT id, file_name, overall_status, check_fail_count, run_timestamp    FROM snapshots    WHERE dataset_name = 'customer_accounts'    ORDER BY id DESC    LIMIT 10")  # Column-level stats for the most recent run dbGetQuery(con,   "SELECT column_name, dq_check, value, threshold    FROM column_snapshots    WHERE snapshot_id = (      SELECT MAX(id) FROM snapshots WHERE dataset_name = 'customer_accounts'    )    ORDER BY column_name, dq_check")  dbDisconnect(con) dbGetQuery(con,   "SELECT dataset_name, COUNT(*) AS runs,           SUM(check_fail_count) AS total_failures    FROM snapshots    GROUP BY dataset_name")

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: run_dq_check() compares current file previous file single run. longer-range comparisons — “dataset’s quality changed past two years?” — use compare_snapshots(), reads two historical snapshots SQLite without needing original files.

code: drift <- compare_snapshots("customer_accounts",                            snapshot_id_prev = 1,                            snapshot_id_curr = 7,                            config_dir = "config",                            report = FALSE)   # skip HTML output  # Table-level summary drift$table_drift  # Schema changes only drift$schema_changes  # Columns with the largest missing-rate shift head(drift$missing_rate_changes[, c("Column", "missing_rate_prev",                                     "missing_rate_curr",                                     "missing_rate_change_pp",                                     "missing_rate_exceeds")])  # Columns whose numeric mean shifted most head(drift$mean_shifts[, c("Column", "numeric_mean_prev",                             "numeric_mean_curr",                             "numeric_mean_shift_pct",                             "numeric_mean_exceeds")])

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: HTML report — one file per run, self-contained (internet required view). Contains: header showing dataset name, file name, run time, overall status badge (green = clear, amber = warnings, red = failures). file summary (row count, column count, file size, format, encoding). quality checks table listing every check, status, observed, threshold, plain-English explanation. Failures warnings appear first. custom checks table (configured). version comparison section showing changed since previous delivery, plain-English narrative summary top. historical trend section showing row count check outcomes across last 10 runs (appears least two runs recorded). appendix full column-level statistics table. text: Global config — dqcheckr.yml Per-dataset config — <dataset_name>.yml
code: snapshot_db: "data/snapshots.sqlite" report_output_dir: "reports/"  default_rules:   # Single-snapshot thresholds   max_missing_rate: 0.05            # FAIL if column missing rate exceeds this   max_non_numeric_rate: 0.01        # FAIL if non-numeric rate in numeric column exceeds this   min_row_count: 0                  # FAIL if row count below this (0 = disabled)    # Version comparison thresholds   max_row_count_change_pct: 0.10   max_numeric_mean_shift_pct: 0.20   max_missing_rate_change_pp: 2.0   # percentage points   max_non_numeric_rate_change_pp: 1.0    # Schema flags   flag_new_columns: true            # set false to suppress new-column warnings in CP-02   flag_dropped_columns: true        # set false to suppress dropped-column warnings in CP-02   flag_type_changes: true           # set false to suppress type-change warnings in CP-02   flag_column_order_change: true    # set false to skip CP-08 entirely (WARN CSV / FAIL FWF) dataset_name: "customer_accounts" folder: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/" format: csv                 # csv | fwf encoding: "UTF-8" delimiter: ","              # csv only fwf_widths: ~               # fwf only fwf_col_names: ~            # fwf only fwf_skip: 0                 # fwf only — header rows to skip (default 0)  # Optional: explicit file paths override folder-scan version detection # current_file: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260410.csv" # previous_file: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260401.csv"  # Optional: schema contract expected_columns:   - id   - name   - country_code   - account_status   - account_balance   - created_date  # Optional: key columns that must be unique key_columns:   - id  # Optional rule overrides (merged over global defaults) rule_overrides:   max_missing_rate: 0.02   max_row_count_change_pct: 0.05   max_non_numeric_rate: 0.005   min_row_count: 1000  # Optional per-column rules column_rules:   country_code:     allowed_values: ["GB", "US", "DE", "FR"]     pattern: "^[A-Z]{2}$"   account_status:     allowed_values: ["ACTIVE", "CLOSED", "SUSPENDED"]   account_balance:     min_value: 0     max_value: 1000000  # Optional custom checks custom_checks_file: "custom/customer_accounts_checks.R"

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Two modes: Folder scan (default): two recently modified files folder used. Newest = current, second-newest = previous. Explicit naming: current_file (optionally previous_file) set dataset config, paths used directly. Useful file naming conventions reflect delivery order, re-running check specific pair files. current file available (either via folder scan explicit config), run proceeds single-file mode: version comparison vs-previous schema checks skipped.

title: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
text: Automated data quality checks recurring dataset deliveries. new file arrival, dqcheckr runs single-snapshot quality checks, compares file previous delivery, writes self-contained HTML report, records summary statistics local SQLite database long-term trend tracking. Supports CSV fixed-width (FWF) formats. Custom organisation-specific checks can supplied plain R files.

title: dqcheckr 0.2.0 — Implementation Plan
text: Motivation (log): RBB-Extended datasets columns like LandlordUnit, TenantPhone, postcode columns predominantly numeric value semantically character. automatic type inference (threshold 90%) classifies numeric, causing: - CP-02 WARN type change non-numeric rate drifts across threshold year--year - CP-04 false mean-shift WARNs phone numbers - CP-07 false non-numeric rate WARNs - QC-11 non-numeric flags valid values like “Unit 3A” Design: column_types map dataset YAML overrides inference per column. cleanest implementation single helper resolve_col_type(col, x, config) checks config$column_types[[col]] first, falls back infer_col_type(). keeps change localised — every function currently calls infer_col_type() directly changed call resolve_col_type() instead, passing column name config. Scope effect: column forced character: - QC-06 reports overridden type (inferred) - QC-07 (numeric stats) skips - QC-11 (non-numeric rate) skips - CP-02 uses override type comparison (eliminates false type-change WARNs) - CP-04 (mean shift) skips - CP-07 (non-numeric rate change) skips - CP-05/CP-06 (new/dropped distinct values) treat character - compute_col_stats() stores overridden type inferred_type SQLite drift comparisons also see override forced numeric (reverse case — forcing mixed column numeric): - QC-07, QC-11, CP-04, CP-07 run numeric - Useful column low non-numeric rate know errors forced date: universal checks (missing rate, distinct count) apply. numeric stats, non-numeric checks. Ambiguities resolve: 1. Valid values character, numeric, date — package validate value abort invalid types? Yes — fail loudly load_config() resolve_col_type(). 2. date override: feature request describes gives example. Treat symmetrically — date-forced column skipped numeric character checks, consistent inferred dates handled. 3. compute_col_stats() currently calls infer_col_type() directly branches col_type == "numeric". needs call resolve_col_type() instead SQLite snapshot records correct type.

title: dqcheckr 0.2.0 — Implementation Plan
text: Motivation (log): LandlordEmail 99% missing design (system doesn’t require ). global 5% missing threshold always FAILs. Similarly BondAmount 0% missing global 5% threshold lenient. Design: Extend existing column_rules map (already used min_value, max_value, pattern, allowed_values) threshold keys. Resolution order: Implement helper col_threshold(config, col, key, default) encodes three-level fallback. Supported threshold keys checks affect: Backwards compatibility: Entirely additive. Configs without per-column thresholds unchanged. Ambiguities resolve: 1. QC-11 currently computes single global threshold numeric columns. per-column thresholds, function must look threshold inside column loop. changes internal structure check_non_numeric() slightly. 2. CP-03, CP-04, CP-07 already per-column loops; threshold lookup becomes one-liner inside loop. 3. max_missing_rate CP-03 vs max_missing_rate_change_pp? different keys different checks — max_missing_rate governs QC-01 (absolute rate), max_missing_rate_change_pp governs CP-03 (change rate). column can set independently. ambiguity clearly named. 4. threshold reported dq_result object reflect effective threshold (per-column one set), HTML report accurate.
code: column_rules.<col>.<threshold>  >  rule_overrides.<threshold>  >  default_rules.<threshold>

NULL, package defaults used (max_missing_rate_change_pp = 2.0, etc.). makes function usable without YAML files present (e.g. files archived). IDs explicit, respect caller order regardless numerically larger. 2. drift HTML report uses kableExtra — already declared dependency. 3. write_drift_html_report() currently writes tempdir() copies. rmarkdown::render() renders template directory default. Keep pattern safety. 4. compare_snapshots() produce text report default? — HTML (matching approach run_dq_check()). Text report available option internal utility. Add text_report = FALSE parameter.

title: dqcheckr 0.2.0 — Implementation Plan
text: DESCRIPTION: Version: 0.2.0 NEWS.md: Add section: cran-comments.md: Update 0.2.0 resubmission (R CMD check passes).
code: # dqcheckr 0.2.0  ## New features  * Per-column type overrides (`column_types` in dataset YAML). Columns can be   forced to `character`, `numeric`, or `date` regardless of inferred type.   Eliminates false QC-11, CP-02, CP-04, and CP-07 findings on phone numbers,   postcodes, unit numbers, and similar semantically-character columns.  * Per-column threshold overrides (`column_rules` extensions). QC-01, QC-11,   CP-03, CP-04, and CP-07 now support per-column thresholds that override the   global/dataset threshold for specific columns.  * `compare_snapshots()` — compare any two historical snapshots from the SQLite   database by ID. Produces a structured list and optional HTML drift report   without requiring the original files.  * `list_snapshots()` — list available snapshots in the database, optionally   filtered by dataset name.

code: # \donttest{ tmp     <- tempdir() db_path <- file.path(tmp, "snap.sqlite") cfg_yml <- file.path(tmp, "dqcheckr.yml") ds_yml  <- file.path(tmp, "starwars_csv.yml") dat     <- system.file("demonstrations/data/starwars.csv", package = "dqcheckr") writeLines(c(   paste0('snapshot_db: "', db_path, '"'),   paste0('report_output_dir: "', tmp, '"'),   'default_rules:',   '  max_missing_rate: 0.60',   '  min_row_count: 80' ), cfg_yml) writeLines(c(   'dataset_name: "starwars_csv"',   paste0('current_file: "', dat, '"'),   'format: csv',   'encoding: "UTF-8"',   'delimiter: ","' ), ds_yml) run_dq_check("starwars_csv", config_dir = tmp, open_report = FALSE) #> [dqcheckr] starwars_csv: FAIL - 0 warning(s), 2 failure(s). Report: /private/tmp/claude-501/Rtmp9SyEfY/starwars_csv_20260530_125808.html run_dq_check("starwars_csv", config_dir = tmp, open_report = FALSE) #> [dqcheckr] starwars_csv: FAIL - 0 warning(s), 2 failure(s). Report: /private/tmp/claude-501/Rtmp9SyEfY/starwars_csv_20260530_125808.html drift <- compare_snapshots("starwars_csv", config_dir = tmp, report = FALSE) #> [dqcheckr] drift: starwars_csv snapshot #1 vs #2 names(drift) #> [1] "dataset_name"         "snap_prev"            "snap_curr"            #> [4] "table_drift"          "schema_changes"       "missing_rate_changes" #> [7] "non_numeric_changes"  "mean_shifts"          "distinct_changes"     # }

what: dqcheckr: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries — dqcheckr-package
title: dqcheckr: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries — dqcheckr-package
text: Automates quality verification recurring external dataset deliveries. new file arrival, runs single-snapshot quality checks (QC-01 QC-14, SC-01/SC-02), compares file previous delivery (CP-01 CP-08), writes self-contained 'HTML' report, records summary statistics local 'SQLite' database long-term trend tracking. Supports 'CSV' fixed-width formats. Custom organisation-specific checks can supplied plain R files.

title: dqcheckr: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries — dqcheckr-package
text: main entry point run_dq_check. Configuration driven two 'YAML' files: global dqcheckr.yml per-dataset <dataset_name>.yml.

title: dqcheckr 0.2.0
text: Per-column type overrides (column_types dataset YAML). column can forced character, numeric, date regardless data looks like. Eliminates false QC-11, CP-02, CP-04, CP-07 findings columns numerically formatted semantically character (phone numbers, postcodes, unit numbers, BSB codes). new resolve_col_type() function exported custom check scripts can also respect overrides. Per-column threshold overrides (new keys column_rules). QC-01 (max_missing_rate), QC-11 (max_non_numeric_rate), CP-03 (max_missing_rate_change_pp), CP-04 (max_numeric_mean_shift_pct), CP-07 (max_non_numeric_rate_change_pp) now accept per-column threshold values column_rules. Resolution order: per-column > dataset (rule_overrides) > global (default_rules). Existing configs without per-column keys unchanged. compare_snapshots() compares two historical snapshots ‘SQLite’ database ID, without needing original files. Produces table-level drift, schema drift, per-column statistical drift. Renders self-contained ‘HTML’ drift report; plain-text report available via text_report = TRUE. Thresholds report output directory read dqcheckr.yml. list_snapshots() lists available snapshots database, optionally filtered dataset name. Returns data frame invisibly.

title: dqcheckr 0.1.1
text: flag_new_columns, flag_dropped_columns, flag_type_changes (CP-02) flag_column_order_change (CP-08) now honoured. Setting flag false suppresses corresponding check report. Schema changes still tracked SQLite snapshot regardless flags. type_inference_threshold now configurable per dataset via rule_overrides dataset YAML (default_rules global config). Previously fixed 90%, now defaults 90% set. Affects QC-06, QC-07, QC-08, QC-11, CP-02, CP-04, CP-05, CP-06, CP-07.

title: dqcheckr 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Single-snapshot quality checks: QC-01 QC-14 (missing rate, empty columns, duplicate rows, row/column counts, inferred types, numeric stats, distinct counts, allowed values, numeric bounds, non-numeric rate, key uniqueness, regex pattern, minimum row count) SC-01/SC-02 (schema contract). Version comparison checks: CP-01 CP-08 (row count change, schema diff, missing rate change, numeric mean shift, new/dropped distinct values, non-numeric rate change, column order). Custom organisation-specific checks via plain R file. Self-contained HTML report check tables, historical trend charts, column statistics appendix. SQLite snapshot database long-term trend tracking. Supports CSV fixed-width (FWF) file formats. Configuration via global dqcheckr.yml per-dataset YAML files.
